materials.material: Carboxylate-functionalized cellulose nanospheres cannot be represented by a simple distinct formula

steps.description: Add (Et4N)2[Fe-(Ph,Me)bTAML] (complex 1, 0.833 mg, 0.001 mmol, 1.67 mol% with respect to cellobiose monomer) and microcrystalline cellulose (20 mg, 0.06 mmol of monomer) to a mortar and grind well.

Iteratively add sodium percarbonate (SPC, 5 eqv. each time with respect to catalyst) to the well-ground mixture with continuous grinding by pestle until the catalyst color bleaches out. Total oxidant added is 2 eqv. of cellulose monomer, added in equal portions over 1.5 h.
After completion of the reaction, dissolve the reaction mixture in water.
Dialyze the aqueous solution through a membrane of 3.5 kDa molecular weight cut-off to remove smaller impurities.
Lyophilize the dialyzed water to obtain the product (carboxylate-functionalized cellulose nanospheres).

equipment.name: mixer mill
equipment.settings: MM 400, 1.5 ml stainless steel jar, 5 mm stainless steel ball, 30 Hz, 10 min per cycle

synthesis.notes: The reaction can be performed either by manual grinding in a mortar and pestle or by mechanical milling in a Retsch MM400 mixer mill. For ball milling: cellulose (20 mg, 0.06 mmol of monomer) and complex 1 (0.43 mg, 0.0005 mmol, 0.9 mol% with respect to cellobiose monomer) are added to a 1.5 ml stainless steel jar with a 5 mm stainless steel ball; SPC (total 2 eqv. of monomer) is added in equal portions over 1.5 h; each cycle is 10 min at 30 Hz. The carboxylate content was calculated to be 1.53 mmol/g by conductometric titration. Crystallinity index (CI) was 31.6% for oxidized cellulose nanospheres vs 80.3% for starting microcrystalline cellulose. Particle size ~100-150 nm by DLS; SEM: 150.5±22.9 nm; TEM: 85.3±15.9 nm; AFM diameter: 89.6±29.9 nm, thickness ~12-15 nm. The same procedure was also applied to lignocellulosic biomass (corncob, softwood pine) with 1.67 mol% catalyst and 5 eqv. SPC per addition over 2.5 h.

steps.description: Add (Et4N)2[Fe-(Ph,Me)bTAML] (1) (0.833 mg, 0.001 mmol, 1.67 mol% with respect to cellobiose monomer) and cellulose (20 mg, 0.06 mmol of monomer) to a mortar or stainless steel ball mill jar.

equipment.name: ball mill
equipment.settings: MM 400, 1.5 ml stainless steel jar, 5 mm stainless steel ball

steps.description: Add sodium percarbonate (SPC, Na2CO3.1.5H2O2) iteratively (5 eqv. each time with respect to catalyst for mortar grinding; total 2 eqv. of monomer added in equal portions over 1.5 h for ball mill) with continuous grinding or milling until the catalyst color bleaches out. Each ball mill cycle: 10 min at 30 Hz frequency.
After completion of the reaction (1.5 h), dissolve the reaction mixture in water.
Dialyze the aqueous mixture through a membrane of 3.5 kDa molecular weight cut-off to remove smaller impurities.
Lyophilize the dialyzed water to obtain the product (carboxylate-functionalized cellulose nanospheres).

equipment.name: ball mill
equipment.settings: MM 400, 1.5 ml stainless steel jar, 5 mm stainless steel ball, 30 Hz

synthesis.notes: The reaction is solvent-free and carried out at room temperature. The catalyst (Et4N)2[Fe-(Ph,Me)bTAML] acts as a combined mimic of LPMO and lignin peroxidase. The resulting CNS had a size of ~100-150 nm (DLS), 150.5±22.9 nm (SEM), 85.3±15.9 nm (TEM), and 89.6±29.9 nm diameter with ~12-15 nm thickness (AFM). Crystallinity index was 31.6% for oxidized CNS vs 80.3% for starting microcrystalline cellulose. The process can also be applied to lignocellulosic biomass (corncob, softwood pine) directly.

steps.description: Add (Et4N)2[Fe-(Ph,Me)bTAML] (1) (0.0005 mmol, 1 mol%) and cellobiose (17.4 mg, 0.05 mmol) to a mortar and grind well.
Add sodium percarbonate (Na2CO3.1.5H2O2) iteratively (2 eqv. each time with respect to catalyst) with continuous grinding by pestle until the catalyst color bleaches out.
Continue grinding until the reaction is completed within 30-45 min.
Dissolve the reaction mixture in water and check by 13C NMR.

synthesis.notes: Cellobiose (C12H22O12) oxidation under solvent-free mechanochemical conditions with (Et4N)2[Fe-(Ph,Me)bTAML] catalyst (1 mol%) and sodium percarbonate as oxidant yields cellobionic acid and gluconic acid in a 4:1 ratio. Oxidation occurs at the anomeric C1 and bridged C4 positions, similar to LPMO reactivity. Products confirmed by 13C NMR and HRMS. Cellobionic acid: 65% yield (13.64 mg); gluconic acid: 16% yield (3.89 mg) as quantified by UV-Vis spectroscopy via copper complexation.
